title: EEG and motion capture data set for a full-body/joystick rotation task
description: This dataset comprises simultaneous EEG and motion capture recordings from 20 healthy adults performing a spatial orientation task involving full-body heading changes. Participants completed a spot rotation task under two conditions: joystick-controlled visual rotation on a 2D display and physical full-body rotation in a virtual reality environment. The dataset includes 157-channel EEG data (129 scalp + 28 neck electrodes) sampled at 1000 Hz and multi-camera motion capture data at 90 Hz, enabling investigation of cortical dynamics and sensorimotor integration during naturalistic spatial navigation.
methods_description: EEG was recorded from 157 active electrodes (129 scalp, 28 neck) at 1000 Hz using a BrainAmp Move System with 0.016-500 Hz band-pass filtering. Motion capture employed 19 active LEDs tracked by 31 cameras (Impulse X2 System) at 90 Hz, supplemented by inertial measurement units on the head and torso. Participants performed a self-paced spot rotation task in two conditions: joystick-controlled visual rotation on a 2D display and physical full-body rotation in a head-mounted VR display (HTC Vive), each preceded by a 3-minute baseline recording.
